content.json:
# Quivrr Operations Dashboard

Use this workbook with the Quivrr production Log Analytics workspace and App Service telemetry.

Primary views:
- Regional overview
- MFA health
- Retailer health
- Inventory counts
- Search quality
- Coverage gaps
- Alert summary

Current SQL-built linkage and inventory metrics come from `python scripts/observability/build_operations_dashboard_metrics.py`.

Search latency and runtime failures should be queried from Log Analytics.

content.query:
AppTraces
| extend payload = parse_json(Message)
| where tostring(payload.service) == "inventory_link_health"
| where tostring(payload.event) == "inventory_link_health_snapshot"
| extend region = tostring(payload.region)
| where '{region}' == 'All' or region == '{region}'
| project timestamp=todatetime(payload.generated_at_utc), region, total_rows=toint(payload.total_rows), linked_model_pct=todouble(payload.linked_model_pct), canonical_size_linked_pct=todouble(payload.canonical_size_linked_pct), retailer_count=toint(payload.retailer_count)
| order by timestamp desc
content.title: Inventory Link Health

content.query:
AppTraces
| extend payload = parse_json(Message)
| where tostring(payload.service) == "quivrr-api"
| where tostring(payload.event) == "search_request_complete"
| extend region = tostring(payload.region)
| where '{region}' == 'All' or region == '{region}'
| summarize requests=count(), avgLatencyMs=avg(todouble(payload.totalDurationMs)), p95LatencyMs=percentile(todouble(payload.totalDurationMs), 95), timeouts=countif(tostring(payload.errorType) has "Timeout"), otherModelMatches=sum(toint(payload.otherModelMatchesCount)) by region
| order by region asc
content.title: Search Health

content.json:
## Deployment Notes

- Import this workbook JSON into Azure Monitor Workbooks.
- Point the KQL queries at the Quivrr production Log Analytics workspace.
- Use the SQL metric builder output as the operator truth for current counts and linkage quality.
- Add SQL-native tiles later if an Azure Workbook data source is introduced for direct SQL rendering.
